author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xix. 28, ‘Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xix. 28, ‘Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We heard in the Gospel that the Lord, rejoicing greatly in Spirit, said unto God the Father, “I confess to Thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because Thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Even so, Father: for so it seemed good in Thy sight.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All things are delivered unto Me of My Father: and no man knoweth the Son, but the Father; neither knoweth any man the Father, save the Son, and he to whomsoever the Son will reveal Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have labour in talking, you in hearing: let us then both give ear to Him who goes on to say, “Come unto Me, all ye that labour.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For why do we labour all, except that we are mortal men, frail creatures and infirm, bearing about vessels of clay which crowd and straiten one another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if these vessels of flesh are straitened, let the open expanse of charity be enlarged.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then does He mean by, “Come unto Me, all ye that labour,” but that ye may labour no more?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In a word, His promise is clear enough; forasmuch as He called those who were in labour, they might perchance enquire, for what profit they were called: “and,” saith He, “I will refresh you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Take My yoke upon you, and learn of Me;” not to raise the fabric of the world, not to create all things visible and invisible, not in the world so created to work miracles and raise the dead; but, “that I am meek and lowly in heart.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wishest to be great, begin from the least.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art thinking to construct some mighty fabric in height; first think of the foundation of humility.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how great soever a mass of building one may wish and design to place above it, the greater the building is to be, the deeper does he dig his foundation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The building in the course of its erection, rises up on high, but he who digs its foundation, must first go down very low.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then you see even a building is low before it is high, and the top is raised only after humiliation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is the top in the erection of that building which we are constructing?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whither will the highest point of this building reach?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I say at once, even to the Vision of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye see how high, how great a thing it is to see God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whoso longeth after it, understands both what I say and what he hears.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Vision of God is promised to us, of the very God, the Supreme God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this is good, to see Him who seeth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they who worship false gods, see them easily; but they see them “who have eyes and see not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But to us is promised the Vision of the Living and the Seeing God, that we may desire eagerly to see that God of whom Scripture saith, “He that planted the ear, shall he not hear?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He that formed the eye, doth he not consider?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Doth He then not hear, who hath made for thee that whereby thou hearest? and doth not He see, who hath created that whereby thou seest?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Well therefore in the foregoing words of this very Psalm doth He say, “Understand therefore ye unwise among the people, and ye fools at length be wise.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For many men commit evil deeds whilst they think they are not seen by God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And it is difficult indeed for them to believe that He cannot see them; but they think that He will not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Few are found of such great impiety, that that should be fulfilled in them which is written, “The fool hath said in his heart, There is no God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is but the madness of a few.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as great piety belongs but to the few, no less also does great impiety.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the multitude of men speak thus: What! is God thinking now upon this, that He should know what I am doing in my house, and does God care for what I may choose to do upon my bed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who says this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Understand, ye unwise among the people, and ye fools at length be wise.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because as being a man, it is a labour for thee to know all that takes place in thy house, and for all the doings and words of thy servants to reach thee; thinkest thou that it is a like labour for God to observe thee, who did not labour to create thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Doth not He fix His eye upon thee, who made thine eye?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wast not, and He created thee and gave thee being; and doth not He care for thee now that thou art, who “calleth those things which be not as though they were”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not then promise thyself this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whether thou wilt or no, He seeth thee, and there is no place whither thou canst hide thyself from His eyes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For if thou goest up into heaven, He is there; if thou goest down into hell, He is there also.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Great is thy labour, whilst unwilling to depart from evil deeds: yet wishest not to be seen by God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hard labour truly!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Daily art thou wishing to do evil, and dost thou suspect that thou art not seen?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Scripture which saith, “He that planted the ear, shall He not hear?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He that formed the eye, doth not He consider?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where canst thou hide thy evil deeds from the eyes of God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou wilt not depart from them, thy labour is great indeed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear Him then who saith, “Come unto Me, all ye that labour.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou canst not end thy labour by flying.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou choose to fly from Him, and not rather to Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Find out then whither thou canst escape, and so fly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if thou canst not fly from Him, for that He is everywhere present; fly (it is quite nigh) to God, who is present where thou art standing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo in thy flight thou hast passed the heavens, He is there; thou hast descended into hell, He is there; whatever deserts of the earth thou shalt choose, there is He, who hath said, “I fill heaven and earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then He fills heaven and earth, and there is no place whither thou canst fly from Him; cease this thy labour, and fly to His presence, lest thou feel His coming.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take courage from the hope that thou shalt by well-living see Him, by whom even in thy evil living thou art seen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in evil living thou canst be seen, thou canst not see; but by well-living thou art both seen and seest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For with how much more tender nearness will He who crowneth the worthy look on thee, who in His pity saw thee that He might call thee when unworthy?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nathanael said to the Lord whom as yet he did not know, “Whence knewest thou me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord said unto him, “When thou wast under the fig-tree I saw thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ saw thee in thine own shade; and will He not see thee in His Light?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what is, “When thou wast under the fig-tree I saw thee”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What does it mean?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Call to mind the original sin of Adam, in whom we all die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When he first sinned, he made himself aprons of fig-leaves, signifying by these leaves the irritations of lust to which he had been reduced by sinning.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hence are we born; in this condition are we born; born in sinful flesh, which “the likeness of sinful flesh” alone can cure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore “God sent His own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He came of this flesh, but He came not as other men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Virgin conceived Him not by lust, but by faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He came into the Virgin, who was before the Virgin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He made choice of her whom He created, He created her whom He designed to choose.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He brought to the Virgin fruitfulness: He took not away her unimpaired purity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then who came to thee without the irritation of the leaves of the fig-tree, “when thou wast under the fig-tree,” saw thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Make ready then to see Him in His height of glory, by whom in His pity thou wast seen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But because the top is high, think of the foundation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What foundation? dost thou say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Learn of Him, for He is meek and lowly in heart.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dig this foundation of lowliness deep in thee, and so wilt thou attain to the crowning top of charity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Turning to the Lord,” etc.
